name: Strategy & Tactics Tree
description: Break an objective into strategy, tactics, and supporting actions
systemPromptHint: Strategy defines the approach; tactics define execution — if a node can be read as both, it is mis-leveled. Objectives at the top must be measurable outcomes, never activities. Sibling tactics must be independent and collectively sufficient to execute the strategy. A strategy without tactics is a wish; tactics that all appear equally important indicate a strategy that lacks clarity.

nodeTags.name: Objective
nodeTags.description: The outcome you want to achieve

nodeTags.name: Strategy
nodeTags.description: A high-level approach for achieving the objective

nodeTags.name: Tactic
nodeTags.description: A concrete action or execution pattern that supports the strategy

derivedIndicators.name: Top-Level Aim
derivedIndicators.description: No incoming edges — a top-level direction or objective

derivedIndicators.name: Bridge
derivedIndicators.description: Has both incoming and outgoing edges — links strategy to execution

derivedIndicators.name: Execution Point
derivedIndicators.description: No outgoing edges — a final action or implementation point
